vulnerability.reason: generated describes a system design flaw (absent safeguards) while reference describes human cognitive overload; different sensitivity conditions
vulnerability.reference: High cognitive load on clinic staff reduces capacity to manually detect scheduling conflicts.
vulnerability.generated: Clinic scheduling portals lack duplicate-check safeguards when automated agents interpret calendar interfaces, enabling erroneous double-bookings.

impact.reason: both reference and generated describe delays, confusion/conflicts, and wasted staff resources (administrative burden covers wasted clinical resources)
impact.reference: Wasted clinical resources, patient care delays, and appointment confusion.
impact.generated: Double-booked patient slots cause appointment conflicts, healthcare delays, and administrative burden for clinic staff.

dose_response.reason: generated arc includes grounding errors leading to double-bookings but omits the critical human failure link where overburdened staff miss conflicts, substituting a technical safeguard gap
dose_response.reference: When agents misinterpret calendar slots due to grounding errors, overburdened clinic staff fail to detect conflicts, resulting in double-bookings that waste resources and delay patient care.
dose_response.generated: Visual and HTML grounding errors in multimodal web agents, when patients use clinic portals, trigger absent duplicate-check safeguards, leading to double-booked slots, appointment conflicts, healthcare delays, and administrative burden.
